{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Inventory Officer",
  "description": "Eko Maintenance Limited is a rapidly growing Facility Management Company that specializes in the maintenance of high-rise building facilities with an office located in Victoria Island, Lagos.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Inventory Officer

Job Location:&nbsp;Victoria Island, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Responsibilities


	Receive incoming goods, store them appropriately, and ensure they are issued correctly.
	Monitor and manage stock levels to ensure adequate supplies are available.
	Distribute supplies from stock as needed by different departments.
	Keep accurate stock records using both manual and computerized systems.
	Use Excel sheets to coordinate and track inventory functions.
	Monitor the movement of materials and products in and out of the inventory through accurate record-keeping.
	Keep track of goods and supplies within the store, ensuring everything is properly organized and accessible.


Relevant Work Experience / Qualification


	Bachelor&rsquo;s Degree or Higher National Diploma (HND) in Business Administration, Supply Chain Management or related fields
	1 &ndash; 3 years working experience in a related field.
	Proficiency in inventory software, ERP system, Microsoft Suite ( Excel, word, Outlook).


Benefits


	Salary: N170,000 - N200,000 monthly.
	HMO Coverage
	Pension
	Annual Leave.


Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to:&nbsp;recruitment3@ekomaintenance.com&nbsp;using &quot;Inventory Officer&quot; as the subject of the email.",

Inventory Officer Job at Eko Maintenance Limited




        
    

        
        Eko Maintenance Limited is a rapidly growing Facility Management Company that specializes in the maintenance of high-rise building facilities with an office located in Victoria Island, Lagos.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Inventory Officer

Job Location: Victoria Island, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Responsibilities
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	Receive incoming goods, store them appropriately, and ensure they are issued correctly.
	Monitor and manage stock levels to ensure adequate supplies are available.
	Distribute supplies from stock as needed by different departments.
	Keep accurate stock records using both manual and computerized systems.
	Use Excel sheets to coordinate and track inventory functions.
	Monitor the movement of materials and products in and out of the inventory through accurate record-keeping.
	Keep track of goods and supplies within the store, ensuring everything is properly organized and accessible.


Relevant Work Experience / Qualification


	Bachelor’s Degree or Higher National Diploma (HND) in Business Administration, Supply Chain Management or related fields
	1 – 3 years working experience in a related field.
	Proficiency in inventory software, ERP system, Microsoft Suite ( Excel, word, Outlook).


Benefits


	Salary: N170,000 - N200,000 monthly.
	HMO Coverage
	Pension
	Annual Leave.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using "Inventory Officer" as the subject of the email.    

    

        Click here to Apply  
        

    
     




        
        

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Front Desk Officer",
  "description": "Chrisvirgy Apartments is a leading hospitality and serviced apartment provider committed to delivering comfort, excellence, and a memorable guest experience.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Front Desk Officer

Job Location:&nbsp;Abuja (FCT)&nbsp;
Employment Type: Full time

Job Description (Key Responsibilities)


	Oversee daily front office operations, including reception, reservations, and concierge services
	Supervise, train, and schedule front desk staff
	Ensure smooth check-in and check-out processes
	Handle guest complaints, special requests, and service recovery
	Coordinate with housekeeping, F&amp;B, and maintenance departments
	Monitor room availability, occupancy, and reservations
	Ensure accuracy of guest records, billing, and cash handling
	Enforce front office policies, procedures, and service standards
	Prepare operational reports and performance summaries for management.


Job Specification (Skills &amp; Competencies):


	Strong leadership and people-management skills
	Excellent communication and customer service abilities
	Strong problem-solving and conflict-resolution skills
	Ability to work under pressure and manage multiple tasks
	Attention to detail and organizational skills
	Proficiency in hotel PMS systems and Microsoft Office
	Professional appearance and attitude.


Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should submit their CV and a cover letter to:&nbsp;career@chrisvirgy.com&nbsp;using &ldquo;Front Desk Officer&ndash; Abuja Hotel.&rdquo;&nbsp;as the subject of the email.",

Front Desk Officer Job at Chrisvirgy Apartments




        
    

        
        Chrisvirgy Apartments is a leading hospitality and serviced apartment provider committed to delivering comfort, excellence, and a memorable guest experience.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Front Desk Officer

Job Location: Abuja (FCT) 
Employment Type: Full time

Job Description (Key Responsibilities)
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	Oversee daily front office operations, including reception, reservations, and concierge services
	Supervise, train, and schedule front desk staff
	Ensure smooth check-in and check-out processes
	Handle guest complaints, special requests, and service recovery
	Coordinate with housekeeping, F&B, and maintenance departments
	Monitor room availability, occupancy, and reservations
	Ensure accuracy of guest records, billing, and cash handling
	Enforce front office policies, procedures, and service standards
	Prepare operational reports and performance summaries for management.


Job Specification (Skills & Competencies):


	Strong leadership and people-management skills
	Excellent communication and customer service abilities
	Strong problem-solving and conflict-resolution skills
	Ability to work under pressure and manage multiple tasks
	Attention to detail and organizational skills
	Proficiency in hotel PMS systems and Microsoft Office
	Professional appearance and attitude.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should submit their CV and a cover letter to: [email protected] using “Front Desk Officer– Abuja Hotel.” as the subject of the email.    

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Procurement Manager",
  "description": "AACE Foods is into food processing, packaging, marketing and distribution of nutritious and tasty products made from the best of West Africa&rsquo;s fruits, herbs, vegetables and cereals. The main product lines consist of spices, spreads, sauces and complimentary food that excite and satisfy institutional, commercial and retail customers across Nigeria.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position:&nbsp;Procurement Manager

Job Location:&nbsp;Lagos&nbsp;&nbsp;
Job Type: Full-time

Job Summary


	In this role, the procurement manager would manage the availability of raw materials, packaging materials, office supplies, and all other procurement activities of the organization.


Responsibilities
Accountable and responsible for:


	Overseeing the sourcing of competitively priced raw materials for AACE Foods&rsquo; operations.
	Liaising with smallholder farmers, clusters, and aggregators of raw materials for AACE Foods&rsquo; production
	Developing relationships with new suppliers
	Tracking and monitoring the impact of AACE&rsquo;s engagement with smallholder farmers and actors across the agricultural value chain.
	Preparing weekly, bi-monthly, and quarterly reports for the AACE Foods team and all partners.
	Working closely with the senior management to prepare reports for development partners and to actively meet reporting requirements.
	Working closely with the Production manager and senior management team to manage the stocks, in-flow, and outflow of inventory and finished goods.
	Interfacing with staff, providing direction and support in all aspects of the production of processed and semi-processed inputs.
	Analyzing material spending to identify trends, leakage, and opportunities for cost reduction, standardization, and best practices.
	Carrying out market surveys, forecasts, and recommendations (regional and international).
	Suppliers&rsquo; performance management and risk assessment on all contracts/agreements.
	Contract management.


Education and Experience


	Minimum of Bachelor&rsquo;s Degree or equivalent in Agriculture or Agribusiness. A degree in Agric Extension would be an added advantage.
	Any related professional affiliation is an added advantage.
	Minimum of 5 years of relevant work experience.
	Previous experience in the private sector (company or association) or with public-private partnerships would be a plus.


Skills and Knowledge:


	Up-to-date knowledge of the agricultural sector in Nigeria, and value chains related to Chili, Ginger, Turmeric, Maize, Garlic, Onions, etc.
	Good understanding of and work experience in value chain development. Knowledge of inclusive agribusiness models.
	Experience with project result-based planning.
	Proven ability to organize and facilitate capacity-building programs (training, demonstrations, field visits, etc.) and other events for value chain stakeholders.


Method of Applicatio
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to:&nbsp;recruiting@aacefoods.com&nbsp;using the Job Title as the subject of the email.",

Procurement Manager Job at AACE Food Processing & Distribution Limited




        
    

        
        AACE Foods is into food processing, packaging, marketing and distribution of nutritious and tasty products made from the best of West Africa’s fruits, herbs, vegetables and cereals. The main product lines consist of spices, spreads, sauces and complimentary food that excite and satisfy institutional, commercial and retail customers across Nigeria.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Procurement Manager

Job Location: Lagos  
Job Type: Full-time

Job Summary
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	In this role, the procurement manager would manage the availability of raw materials, packaging materials, office supplies, and all other procurement activities of the organization.


Responsibilities
Accountable and responsible for:


	Overseeing the sourcing of competitively priced raw materials for AACE Foods’ operations.
	Liaising with smallholder farmers, clusters, and aggregators of raw materials for AACE Foods’ production
	Developing relationships with new suppliers
	Tracking and monitoring the impact of AACE’s engagement with smallholder farmers and actors across the agricultural value chain.
	Preparing weekly, bi-monthly, and quarterly reports for the AACE Foods team and all partners.
	Working closely with the senior management to prepare reports for development partners and to actively meet reporting requirements.
	Working closely with the Production manager and senior management team to manage the stocks, in-flow, and outflow of inventory and finished goods.
	Interfacing with staff, providing direction and support in all aspects of the production of processed and semi-processed inputs.
	Analyzing material spending to identify trends, leakage, and opportunities for cost reduction, standardization, and best practices.
	Carrying out market surveys, forecasts, and recommendations (regional and international).
	Suppliers’ performance management and risk assessment on all contracts/agreements.
	Contract management.


Education and Experience


	Minimum of Bachelor’s Degree or equivalent in Agriculture or Agribusiness. A degree in Agric Extension would be an added advantage.
	Any related professional affiliation is an added advantage.
	Minimum of 5 years of relevant work experience.
	Previous experience in the private sector (company or association) or with public-private partnerships would be a plus.


Skills and Knowledge:


	Up-to-date knowledge of the agricultural sector in Nigeria, and value chains related to Chili, Ginger, Turmeric, Maize, Garlic, Onions, etc.
	Good understanding of and work experience in value chain development. Knowledge of inclusive agribusiness models.
	Experience with project result-based planning.
	Proven ability to organize and facilitate capacity-building programs (training, demonstrations, field visits, etc.) and other events for value chain stakeholders.


Method of Applicatio
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Title as the subject of the email.    

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Factory Operations Manager (Construction Experience)",
  "description": "Elizabeth Maddeux Our client, a leading chemical manufacturing company, seeks an experienced candidates to lead end-to-end plant operations within a process-driven production environment in the capacity below:

Job Position: Factory Operations Manager (Construction Experience)
Job Location:&nbsp;Sagamu, Ogun
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary


	The Factory Operations Manager will oversee production, process optimization, engineering maintenance, quality control, HSE compliance, and supply chain coordination to ensure efficient, safe, and cost-effective plant performance.
	This role requires strong expertise in process manufacturing, industrial safety standards, and operational excellence within a regulated chemical production environment.


Key Responsibilities
Plant &amp; Production Leadership:


	Lead all daily operations of the chemical manufacturing plant, ensuring production targets, yield efficiency, and quality benchmarks are achieved.
	Oversee batch/process manufacturing systems to maximize throughput and minimize production losses.
	Drive capacity utilization and optimize plant output in line with demand forecasts.


Process Optimization &amp; Continuous Improvement:


	Implement Lean Manufacturing, Six Sigma, and Total Productive Maintenance (TPM) methodologies.
	Reduce waste, improve yield, and enhance operational efficiency through structured process improvement initiatives.
	Monitor KPIs such as OEE (Overall Equipment Effectiveness), downtime, and defect rates.


Maintenance &amp; Engineering Oversight:


	Lead preventive and predictive maintenance programs to minimize equipment failure.
	Ensure reliability of production machinery, utilities, and process systems.
	Collaborate with engineering teams on plant upgrades and automation improvements.


HSE &amp; Regulatory Compliance:


	Enforce strict compliance with environmental, health, and safety regulations applicable to chemical manufacturing.
	Oversee hazardous material handling, storage, and disposal procedures.
	Ensure plant operations comply with regulatory authorities and industry safety standards.


Supply Chain &amp; Resource Management:


	Coordinate with procurement and logistics to ensure uninterrupted availability of raw materials.
	Monitor inventory levels and production planning alignment.
	Control operational costs and manage plant budgets effectively.


Leadership &amp; Workforce Management:


	Lead and develop cross-functional plant teams (production, maintenance, quality, warehouse).
	Foster a strong safety culture and performance accountability. Drive workforce planning aligned with production schedules.


Performance Monitoring &amp; Reporting:


	Analyze plant performance metrics and present operational reports to executive management.
	Develop corrective action plans to address production inefficiencies or quality deviations.
	#Contribute to company-wide strategic planning and operational expansion initiatives.


Qualifications &amp; Experience


	Bachelor&rsquo;s degree in Chemical Engineering, Mechanical Engineering, Industrial Engineering, or related discipline.
	Minimum 8&ndash;10 years of progressive experience in chemical or constructionmanufacturing environments.
	At least 5 years in a senior plant/operations leadership role.
	MBA is an advantage.
	Strong understanding of process manufacturing systems and industrial automation.
	Proven experience managing large production teams and plant budgets.
	Demonstrated implementation of Lean, Six Sigma, or TPM frameworks.


Core Competencies:


	Process manufacturing expertise
	Plant operations strategy
	Industrial safety and regulatory compliance
	Yield optimization &amp; cost control
	Budget management
	Team leadership and performance management
	Data-driven operational decision-making


Salary
N800,000 - N1,000,000 monthly.

Method of Application
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CVs and cover letter to:&nbsp;careers@elizabethmaddeux.com&nbsp;using &lsquo;Factory Operations Manager - Sagamu&#39; as the subject of the email.

Note:&nbsp;Only qualified candidates will be contacted.",

Factory Operations Manager (Construction Experience) Job at Elizabeth Maddeux




        
    

        
        Elizabeth Maddeux Our client, a leading chemical manufacturing company, seeks an experienced candidates to lead end-to-end plant operations within a process-driven production environment in the capacity below:

Job Position: Factory Operations Manager (Construction Experience)
Job Location: Sagamu, Ogun
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	The Factory Operations Manager will oversee production, process optimization, engineering maintenance, quality control, HSE compliance, and supply chain coordination to ensure efficient, safe, and cost-effective plant performance.
	This role requires strong expertise in process manufacturing, industrial safety standards, and operational excellence within a regulated chemical production environment.


Key Responsibilities
Plant & Production Leadership:


	Lead all daily operations of the chemical manufacturing plant, ensuring production targets, yield efficiency, and quality benchmarks are achieved.
	Oversee batch/process manufacturing systems to maximize throughput and minimize production losses.
	Drive capacity utilization and optimize plant output in line with demand forecasts.


Process Optimization & Continuous Improvement:


	Implement Lean Manufacturing, Six Sigma, and Total Productive Maintenance (TPM) methodologies.
	Reduce waste, improve yield, and enhance operational efficiency through structured process improvement initiatives.
	Monitor KPIs such as OEE (Overall Equipment Effectiveness), downtime, and defect rates.


Maintenance & Engineering Oversight:


	Lead preventive and predictive maintenance programs to minimize equipment failure.
	Ensure reliability of production machinery, utilities, and process systems.
	Collaborate with engineering teams on plant upgrades and automation improvements.


HSE & Regulatory Compliance:


	Enforce strict compliance with environmental, health, and safety regulations applicable to chemical manufacturing.
	Oversee hazardous material handling, storage, and disposal procedures.
	Ensure plant operations comply with regulatory authorities and industry safety standards.


Supply Chain & Resource Management:


	Coordinate with procurement and logistics to ensure uninterrupted availability of raw materials.
	Monitor inventory levels and production planning alignment.
	Control operational costs and manage plant budgets effectively.


Leadership & Workforce Management:


	Lead and develop cross-functional plant teams (production, maintenance, quality, warehouse).
	Foster a strong safety culture and performance accountability. Drive workforce planning aligned with production schedules.


Performance Monitoring & Reporting:


	Analyze plant performance metrics and present operational reports to executive management.
	Develop corrective action plans to address production inefficiencies or quality deviations.
	#Contribute to company-wide strategic planning and operational expansion initiatives.


Qualifications & Experience


	Bachelor’s degree in Chemical Engineering, Mechanical Engineering, Industrial Engineering, or related discipline.
	Minimum 8–10 years of progressive experience in chemical or constructionmanufacturing environments.
	At least 5 years in a senior plant/operations leadership role.
	MBA is an advantage.
	Strong understanding of process manufacturing systems and industrial automation.
	Proven experience managing large production teams and plant budgets.
	Demonstrated implementation of Lean, Six Sigma, or TPM frameworks.


Core Competencies:


	Process manufacturing expertise
	Plant operations strategy
	Industrial safety and regulatory compliance
	Yield optimization & cost control
	Budget management
	Team leadership and performance management
	Data-driven operational decision-making


Salary
N800,000 - N1,000,000 monthly.

Method of Application
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CVs and cover letter to: [email protected] using ‘Factory Operations Manager - Sagamu' as the subject of the email.

Note: Only qualified candidates will be contacted.    

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Finance & Administrative Assistant (Entry Level)",
  "description": "At SIM, We are team of professionals and technical specialists from wide ranging backgrounds. We combine the capacity for promoting businesses, delivering services and building organizations as wellas strengthening their systems and have sustained our reputation for improving organizational cultures And business outcomes. Our charter is to focus on evolving innovative solutions for change at affordable costs.
Soft Intelligent Machines (SIM) helps each client determine an achievable course of action that aligns with its current and future business priorities. We help our clients to focus on doing the right things that result in immediate business impact. We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Finance &amp; Administrative Assistant (Entry Level)

Job Location:&nbsp;Ikeja, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Description


	We are seeking a dedicated and detail-oriented Finance &amp; Administrative Assistant to join our growing team.
	This role is ideal for recent graduates or early-career professionals who have a strong interest in finance, office administration, and supporting efficient business operations.
	The successful candidate will assist the finance team with financial transactions, record-keeping, and reporting, while also providing general administrative support to ensure smooth day-to-day operations.


Key Responsibilities


	Assist in maintaining accurate financial records, ledgers, and documentation.
	Process invoices, receipts, payments, and expense claims in a timely and accurate manner.
	Support accounts payable and accounts receivable activities.
	Coordinate office administration, scheduling, and internal correspondence.
	Support day-to-day office operations and ensure smooth administrative processes.
	Monitor and manage office supplies, equipment, and general resources.
	Perform other finance-related and administrative duties as assigned by the Team Lead.


Requirements


	Bachelor&rsquo;s Degree in Accounting, Business Administration, or related field.
	Must have completed NYSC or possess an exemption certificate.
	Proficiency in Microsoft Office Suite
	Strong organizational, multitasking, and record-keeping skills.
	Good communication and interpersonal abilities.
	High level of integrity, attention to detail, and professionalism.


Method of Application&nbsp;
